{{ define "main" }} {{ $docs := site.GetPage "/docs" }} {{ $gettingStarted := site.GetPage "/docs/getting-started" }} {{ $api := site.GetPage "/docs/api" }} {{ $examples := site.GetPage "/docs/examples" }}
deployed_code {{ .Site.Title }}
Docs Getting Started API Examples
Open docs
Stitch-inspired docs refresh

Ship Pixi games from scenes, state, and layout.

Pixora is a declarative-by-default framework for building high-performance Pixi.js interfaces. The docs now mirror the product language with a polished landing page, rich docs shell, and light/dark patterns tuned for real documentation work.

Start with `pixora()` arrow_forward Explore the reference game stadia_controller
quick-start.ts
1 import { pixora } from 'pixora';
2 import { heroScene } from './scenes/hero.scene';
3
4 const runtime = await pixora({
5 mount: document.querySelector('#app')!,
6 initialScene: 'hero',
7 scenes: [heroScene],
8 });
9
10 await runtime.start();

Docs tracks

{{ len $docs.Sections }}

Getting Started, Guides, API Reference, and Examples stay organized as product-grade docs surfaces.

API modules

{{ len $api.RegularPages }}

Core runtime modules are navigable from a dense sidebar and polished reference layout inspired by the Stitch API screen.

Live example

1

The Space Invaders build ships inside the docs output so readers can move between guides and a real game without leaving the site.

Live reference example

The docs artifact now feels like the product.

The landing page, documentation shell, and embedded example all follow the same visual grammar: framed surfaces, glowing purple accents, dense side navigation, and a theme toggle that carries the design into light mode.

  • check_circleClass-based theme switching aligned with the Stitch dark and light screens.
  • check_circleTailwind CLI compilation folded directly into the Nx-driven Hugo build and serve flow.
  • check_circleReusable cards and prose styles that make markdown content look intentionally designed instead of merely skinned.
Open standalone build open_in_new Read the walkthrough description

Why teams start here

A docs site shaped like the framework itself.

The refreshed site highlights how Pixora works: declarative scene orchestration, layout-aware UI, and production-ready examples that keep implementation and documentation in the same flow.

schema

Declarative scenes

Compose screens from render functions and scene transitions instead of building deep imperative state machines first.

instant_mix

Reactive state

Keep HUD state, overlays, and gameplay signals close to the scene that owns them with Pixora's runtime primitives.

view_quilt

Layout-aware UI

Use flex and percent layout helpers to build menus, overlays, and score surfaces without detaching from the rendering engine.

rocket_launch

Deployable examples

Docs and demos ship together so a guide, API page, and working example remain part of one hosted artifact.

Explore the docs

Choose a path through Pixora.

{{ $section.Title }}

{{ $section.Description }}

Ready to build?

Move from first scene to shipped example without leaving the docs.

Start with installation, learn the declarative runtime, then inspect the example app and API modules inside the same polished surface.

Install Pixora download Browse the API api
